description_long: This object consists of a nested set of three rectangular wooden stools, each fitting neatly under the next larger one. The stools are constructed from planks of wood with a visibly weathered, rustic finish, giving them a natural and slightly aged appearance. Each stool has four straight legs and a flat, square top, with simple horizontal supports connecting the legs near the base for added stability. The wood grain and texture are prominent, with a light brown coloration and some variation in tone, suggesting reclaimed or untreated wood. The design is functional and unadorned, emphasizing practicality and stackability for space-saving purposes. These stools would be suitable for use in urban parks, public seating areas, or outdoor cafes.
description: A set of three nested, rustic wooden stools with a weathered finish and simple, sturdy construction. The stools feature square tops, four legs each, and visible wood grain.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stools nested together, highlighting their square tops and the depth of the set.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the height difference between the three stools and their individual leg structures.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizing the alignment of the legs and the nested arrangement from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the staggered heights and the open space beneath each stool.
